title: Kanban Plan
description: Add a rules-first quality layer to ct-cognize so ranking becomes more stable, explainable, and aligned with taste profile hard rules.

planning_session.author: codex
planning_session.goal: Raise taste-selection confidence by combining deterministic taste rules with bounded LLM refinement and preserving quality signals in downstream artifacts.

DONE.task: Implement taste/movie normalization and deterministic rule scoring in ct-cognize before the agent call.
DONE.notes: Completed with rules-first scoring, zero-width-safe normalization, and anime-creator fallback floors for sparse pages.

DONE.task: Replace free-form AI scoring with bounded LLM refinement merged against rule floors, ceilings, and quality confidence.
DONE.notes: Completed with bounded merge, confidence gates, preserved diagnostics, and stable live pi delivery.

DONE.task: Extend analysis/filter contracts and docs so rule_score, llm_delta, confidence, review_required, and decision_basis survive validation and downstream stages.
DONE.notes: Completed across contracts, downstream filter propagation, samples, viz docs, and memory cards.

DONE.task: Add regression coverage and live verification for the rules-first ranking path and strong anime matches.
DONE.notes: Completed with 133 passing targeted tests and live Telegram verification after reruns.

acceptance_criteria: ct-cognize computes a deterministic rule layer before agent refinement
LLM output is merged with bounded delta and cannot violate hard profile rules
analysis-result and filter-result preserve rule_score, llm_delta, confidence, review_required, and decision_basis
Regression tests prove strong anime matches survive and false-positive drift is reduced
One live pipeline run confirms the new scoring path still delivers to Telegram
